stories.headline: US-Israel War Against Iran: Strikes, Casualties, and Escalating Conflict

articles.title: Navy readying aircraft carrier for deployment as Iran conflict deepens
articles.description: The move may raise speculation HMS Prince of Wales could be sent to defend British interests during the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: Dozens killed as Israeli special forces raid Lebanese village in search of 40-year-old remains
articles.description: Overnight, one Israeli operation saw at least 41 people killed and 40 injured, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

articles.title: Iranian ambassador warns UK to be 'very careful' about further involvement in war
articles.description: Seyed Ali Mousavi says Iran has a "right to self-defence" if the UK directly joins US-Israeli attacks.

articles.title: Why did Iran's president apologise?
articles.description: The apology may hint at a political repositioning inside Iran, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azmi.

articles.title: We've been speaking to Iranians during one week of war. Here's what they said
articles.description: Ordinary Iranians reflect on seven days of conflict and where they see their country going next.

articles.title: There's an inflation wave coming - what does the war mean for the UK economy?
articles.description: Economic consequences are an intrinsic aspect of the Iran conflict, writes BBC economics editor Faisal Islam.

articles.title: In maps: Eight days of strikes across the Middle East
articles.description: Israel has continued strikes across Iran and Lebanon and the Iranian regime has carried out more attacks, as the war enters its eighth day.

articles.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
articles.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched wide-ranging strikes on Iran on 28 February.

articles.title: European leaders' views on the developing war in the Middle East
articles.description: Europeans are divided over how to respond to the US/Israel war with Iran. German leaders have been measured, while Spain's prime minister has been critical of the Trump Administration and of Israel.

articles.title: At the border between Iran and Turkey, some flee the war, while others return
articles.description: Iranians are streaming across the border with Turkey, fleeing constant bombardment. But some are also going the other way -- returning to Iran out of worry for loved ones they can't otherwise reach.

articles.title: Trump warns Iran 'will be hit very hard' as war enters its second week
articles.description: Trump warned Saturday that Iran would soon "be hit very hard," adding that the U.S. may expand its target list to "new areas and groups of people."

articles.title: Iran's president apologizes for strikes on neighbors as strikes pound their cities
articles.description: President Masoud Pezeshkian said Saturday that a demand by the U.S. for an unconditional surrender is a "dream that they should take to their grave." He also apologized for Iran's attacks on regional countries.

articles.title: Iran live updates: Trump warns Iran may face 'complete destruction and certain death'
articles.description: President Donald Trump called upon the Iranian Revolutionary Guard, military and police to "lay down their arms" against the U.S. and Israel.

articles.title: Trump exalts progress in Iran war after warning country will be "hit very hard"
articles.description: President Trump warned Iran that it would be "hit very hard," after calling for the country's "unconditional surrender."

articles.title: Trump salutes as 6 soldiers killed in Iran are transferred back to the U.S.
articles.description: All six service members died during an unmanned aircraft system attack in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait.

articles.title: 3/07: Saturday Morning
articles.description: President Trump said on social media that Iran will be "hit very hard" on Saturday after Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian apologized to the country's regional neighbors for retaliatory strikes against Israel and U.S. bases in the Middle East.

articles.title: Iran's president apologizes to Persian Gulf countries for retaliatory strikes
articles.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian released a video apology to Iran's neighbors for the retaliatory strikes against Israel and U.S. bases in the Middle East, saying they will stop striking other Persian Gulf states unless attacked first. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab has more from Doha.

articles.title: U.S. and Israel hit Iran with more attacks
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel's war against Iran entered its eighth day, with ongoing strikes on Tehran. CBS News chief correspondent Matt Gutman has the latest from Tel Aviv.

articles.title: Hegseth on reports of Russia sharing intelligence with Iran: "No one's putting us in danger"
articles.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth told CBS News' Major Garrett that "no one's putting us in danger" over reports of Russia sharing intelligence with Iran in a 60 Minutes interview airing Sunday, March 8. CBS News national security contributor Samantha Vinograd has more.

articles.title: In War’s First Week, a Punishing Military Campaign With No Coherent Endgame
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel have pounded Iran’s leadership and undercut its defense capabilities, but President Trump has offered wildly different explanations for what he hopes to achieve.

articles.title: Skies above Tehran 'turning orange' amid U.S. strikes
articles.description: NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports that a NBC News producer on the ground in the Iranian capital of Tehran says that as blasts hit the city, the sky is "turning orange." President Donald Trump said in a post on social media hours earlier that Iran would be hit "very hard."

articles.title: A free Iran is their shared dream. But the diaspora remains torn on the best path forward.
articles.description: The mix of joy and anxiety among the Iranian diaspora in recent days reflects the complicated emotions many feel as violence escalates in the Middle East.

articles.title: Camouflage and crudites: Trump wages war and hosts parties at Mar-a-Lago
articles.description: Donald Trump watched “Operation Epic Fury,” the beginning of war with Iran, unfold from a makeshift “situation room" at Mar-a-Lago.

articles.title: What to know about surge in gas, oil prices as Iran turmoil persists
articles.description: The growing conflict in Iran, spearheaded by U.S. and Israeli strikes followed by retaliatory attacks by Iran on Gulf partners, has resulted in a surge in domestic gas and oil prices. The U.S. national average for a gallon of regular gasoline jumped 14 percent since last week to $3.41 as of Saturday, according to data…

articles.title: Most Americans oppose ongoing US military action in Iran: Survey
articles.description: A majority of Americans are opposed to ongoing U.S. military action in Iran even as President Trump forcefully vows to press on with the operation until the Iranian regime surrenders, according to a new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll. The poll, released Friday, found that 56 percent of Americans opposed or strongly opposed military action in the…

articles.title: Iran’s foreign minister: If Trump ‘seeks escalation’ that’s ‘what he will get’
articles.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Saturday reupped his warning about retaliation against the U.S. as conflict in the Middle East persists after last week’s strikes. “If [President Trump] seeks escalation, it is precisely what our Powerful Armed Forces have long been prepared for, and what he will get,” Aragchi said in a statement shared…

articles.title: Tapper blasts Trump administration for depicting Iran conflict ‘like a game’
articles.description: CNN anchor Jake Tapper blasted the Trump administration on Friday for depicting the ongoing conflict in Iran “like a game” after the White House released clips on social media mixing footage of what appeared to be U.S. military strikes with clips from video games and Hollywood productions. “Well, the last day or so, the White…

articles.title: Maher on support for Iran strikes: ‘I just don’t get what liberals don’t get about liberation’
articles.description: Late-night comedian Bill Maher said he’s “cautiously optimistic” about President Trump’s ongoing military operation against Iran, “unless he puts boots on the ground.” “I just don’t get what liberals don’t get about liberation,” he said during Friday’s episode of “Real Time with Bill Maher,” adding that he sees “so much happiness” from the Iranian people.…

articles.title: Hegseth on reports of Russia helping Iran: ‘No one’s putting us in danger’
articles.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth rebuffed concerns that Russia is supporting Iran amid the U.S.’s ongoing military operation in the Middle East. Hegseth said “no one’s putting us in danger” when asked by CBS News’s Major Garrett about reports that Russia provided information to Iran to help them target U.S. bases in the Middle East.  “We’re…

articles.title: Stagflation? $150 oil? That doesn’t mean everyone’s 401(k) is losing money.
articles.description: The problem with crises like this is that stocks and bonds can both fail.

articles.title: There’s a new record number of 401(k) millionaires — and the Iran conflict will test their discipline
articles.description: The same financial habits that pushed the number of 401(k) millionaires to new highs should help retirement savers through the current global uncertainty

articles.title: American father hears missiles overhead while stranded in Dubai with kids and pregnant wife
articles.description: An American family from Louisiana has been stranded in Dubai as missiles shake their hotel amid the Middle East crisis. They were hopeful about getting out this weekend.

articles.title: Senior Iranian leader issues chilling warning to Trump following continued US-Israeli air strikes: ‘He must pay the price’
articles.description: Ali Larijani, one of Iran’s remaining leaders, issued a chilling warning Saturday that President Trump must “pay the price” – following US and Israeli strikes that have decimated Tehran’s top figures.

articles.title: White House’s bombastic Iran war media blitz breaks from precedent, shocking critics
articles.description: The White House has taken heat from critics over its social media strategy celebrating U.S. military strikes that have successfully hit targets throughout Iran in the first week of the war with the Islamic regime. Between sharing videos of airstrikes referencing the video games Grand Theft Auto and Call of Duty and compilations of strikes […]

articles.title: War with Iran sees several historical firsts
articles.description: The joint U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran in the morning hours of Feb. 28 sparked the largest regional war in the Middle East in decades and marked several historical milestones. Operation Epic Fury is the largest U.S. campaign since its invasion of Iraq in 2003, and is poised to reshape the order in the Middle East […]

articles.title: Iran promises to not attack Middle East neighbors but still faces ‘complete destruction,’ Trump says
articles.description: President Donald Trump said that Iran has “apologized and surrendered” to its neighboring countries in the Middle East, announcing U.S. and Israeli military pressure had led them to this decision. In his social media post made early Saturday morning, Trump called Iran the “LOSER OF THE MIDDLE EAST” and said this would be their reputation […]

articles.title: Trump grapples with the greatest cost of his war in Iran: The human cost
articles.description: President Donald Trump is under pressure to minimize the cost of Operation Epic Fury, his expanding conflict against Iran. The Department of War confirmed this week the identities of the six U.S. service members killed in action so far: Maj. Jeffrey O’Brien, 45, of Iowa; Chief Warrant Officer 3 Robert Marzan, 54, of California; Capt. […]

articles.title: On Iran, Congress Still Has a Say
articles.description: The further we are from an actual threat, the greater is the president’s political need for congressional support.

articles.title: Lessons from the Last Iranian Revolution

analysis.summary: The US and Israel launched wide-ranging strikes on Iran beginning February 28, 2026, in an operation called 'Epic Fury,' now entering its second week. Iran has retaliated with strikes against Israel and US bases in the region, while Iran's president apologized to Gulf neighbors for collateral strikes but rejected US demands for unconditional surrender. The conflict has killed six US service members, caused significant Iranian casualties including reportedly Supreme Leader Khamenei, disrupted global energy markets, and drawn divided reactions domestically and internationally.
agreements: US and Israel launched joint strikes on Iran beginning February 28, 2026, now entering the eighth day
Trump warned on Saturday that Iran would be 'hit very hard'
Iranian President Pezeshkian apologized to neighboring Gulf states for retaliatory strikes hitting their territory
Iran rejected the US demand for unconditional surrender
Six US service members were killed in a drone attack at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait
Gas and oil prices have surged significantly due to the conflict
Thousands of Americans have been evacuated from the Middle East
Reports emerged that Russia provided intelligence to Iran to help target US military assets
Defense Secretary Hegseth dismissed concerns about Russia helping Iran, saying 'no one's putting us in danger'
Strikes on Tehran continued through Saturday night with explosions and fires visible
The US sank an Iranian warship with a submarine-launched torpedo, with Sri Lanka recovering 87 bodies

disagreements.topic: Whether there is a coherent strategy or endgame

positions.position: Reports a 'punishing military campaign with no coherent endgame,' noting Trump has offered 'wildly different explanations' for what he hopes to achieve
Frames the operation as achieving 'historical firsts' and successfully pressuring Iran, noting it is 'poised to reshape the order in the Middle East'
Urges caution and 'applying Iraq's hardest lessons,' warning Iran requires 'a disciplined approach and a clear plan for the future'
Publishes multiple perspectives including framing it as a deliberate 'strategy of decapitation' while also warning 'Iran 2026 is not Iraq 2003'

disagreements.topic: Framing of Trump's rhetoric and White House social media strategy

Analyzes the apology as potentially hinting at 'political repositioning inside Iran'
Notes Iran's judiciary chief contradicted the presidential apology, warning of 'heavy attacks' on areas 'at disposal of enemy'

disagreements.topic: Whether the conflict is primarily America's or Israel's war

Consistently frames it as a joint 'US and Israel' operation with shared responsibility

articles.title: Noem thanks Trump for new Shield of the Americas special envoy role after DHS ouster
articles.description: Kristi Noem thanked President Donald Trump for new Shield of the Americas special envoy role during a summit Saturday, days after he removed her as DHS secretary.

Trump made the comments as part of his remarks at the first Shield of the Americas summit in Doral, Florida, where he also announced a new military […]

Trump said the newly dubbed “Americas Counter Cartel Coalition” includes 17 countries across the Americas and is aimed at curbing drug violence from organized cartels. “Representatives of 17 different […]

articles.title: WATCH LIVE: Trump speaks at Shield of Americas Summit
articles.description: President Donald Trump is set to speak at around 10:00 a.m. at the Shield of the Americas Summit in Doral, Florida. The summit first gained attention on Thursday, when Trump fired Kristi Noem from her role as Homeland Security Secretary and appointed her instead as Special Envoy for The Shield of the Americas. Trump said […]

articles.title: Trump Announces Military Coalition With Latin American Leaders to Eradicate Cartels - theepochtimes.com
articles.description: Trump Announces Military Coalition With Latin American Leaders to Eradicate Cartels  theepochtimes.com

analysis.summary: President Trump hosted the inaugural 'Shield of the Americas' summit in Doral, Florida, where he announced a new military coalition called the 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition' involving 17 countries to combat drug cartels in the Western Hemisphere. Trump also issued warnings to Cuba, suggesting imminent action and indicating Cuba was negotiating a deal with him and Secretary of State Rubio. The summit also featured the appointment of former DHS Secretary Kristi Noem as Special Envoy for the Shield of the Americas.
agreements: Trump held the inaugural Shield of the Americas summit in Doral, Florida on Saturday
Trump announced a new military coalition aimed at eradicating drug cartels in the Western Hemisphere
Trump directed warnings and threats toward Cuba during the summit
Kristi Noem was appointed as Special Envoy for the Shield of the Americas after being removed as DHS Secretary
The coalition involves Latin American and Caribbean nations

disagreements.topic: Framing of Trump's rhetoric toward cartels and Cuba

positions.position: Leads with Trump calling cartels 'cancer' and frames his remarks as threatening, emphasizing his offer to 'launch missiles in Latin America'
Frames Trump's remarks as 'boasting of America's military actions'
Frames the initiative more neutrally as a revamped 'war on drugs'
Frames Trump's Cuba remarks more favorably, quoting him saying 'great change' will come to Cuba
Frames the announcement straightforwardly as a military coalition with Latin American leaders

disagreements.topic: Primary focus of summit coverage

positions.position: Focuses on Bolivia's political U-turn toward the U.S. as the main storyline, highlighting Bolivia's shift after two decades of resisting U.S. influence
Centers the story on Kristi Noem thanking Trump for her new special envoy role after her DHS ouster
Covers both the military coalition announcement and the Cuba negotiations as separate major stories
Covers both Cuba warnings and the counter-cartel coalition as separate stories with equal emphasis

disagreements.topic: Size and specifics of the coalition

positions.position: Specifies the coalition is called the 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition' and includes 17 countries
Describes it as a 'new military coalition' without specifying the number of countries in headline

stories.headline: Kristi Noem Fired as DHS Secretary; Trump Appoints Replacement

articles.title: Kristi Noem Survived Many Crises. Then She Crossed a Trump Red Line.
articles.description: President Trump, who values loyalty, has at times tried to distance himself from his administration’s own actions when they become politically toxic.

articles.title: Trump fires Homeland Security Secretary Noem after mounting criticism over her leadership - AP News

analysis.summary: President Trump fired Kristi Noem as Secretary of Homeland Security amid mounting criticism of her leadership. Following her removal, Noem was appointed to a new role as 'Shield of the Americas' special envoy, which she publicly thanked Trump for at a summit. The move drew cheers from Democrats, who called for additional administration departures.

Frames it as an overdue correction, arguing Noem was never well suited for the job and made the serious role resemble something from the show 'Veep'

stories.headline: Iranian President Pezeshkian Apologizes to Gulf Neighbors, Faces Hardliner Backlash

articles.title: Why did Iran's president apologise?
articles.description: The apology may hint at a political repositioning inside Iran, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azmi.

articles.title: Iran's president apologizes for strikes on neighbors as strikes pound their cities
articles.description: President Masoud Pezeshkian said Saturday that a demand by the U.S. for an unconditional surrender is a "dream that they should take to their grave." He also apologized for Iran's attacks on regional countries.

articles.title: Iran's president apologizes to Persian Gulf countries for retaliatory strikes
articles.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian released a video apology to Iran's neighbors for the retaliatory strikes against Israel and U.S. bases in the Middle East, saying they will stop striking other Persian Gulf states unless attacked first. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab has more from Doha.

articles.title: Iranian President’s Apology to Neighboring Gulf States Showcases Leadership Rifts
articles.description: Masoud Pezeshkian, the Iranian president, apologized for Iranian strikes on Gulf states before backtracking after criticism from other Iranian leaders. Despite his remarks, Iran has continued its attacks.

analysis.summary: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian issued an apology to neighboring Persian Gulf countries for Iranian retaliatory strikes against Israel and U.S. bases in the region. The apology provoked sharp criticism from hardliners within Iran, and reports indicate Pezeshkian subsequently backtracked on some of his remarks. He simultaneously rejected U.S. demands for unconditional surrender.

The US State Department reported evacuating thousands of Americans, with the total reaching approximately 28,000, while the UK announced plans to charter flights for British nationals early next week.

Provides a behind-the-scenes look at how US officials scrambled to arrange evacuations as Iran's retaliation was unfolding, emphasizing the urgency and reactive nature of the government response

Hegseth said “no one’s putting us in danger” when asked by CBS News’s Major Garrett about reports that Russia provided information to Iran to help them target U.S. bases in the Middle East.  “We’re…

stories.headline: Texas Democratic Primary: Talarico Defeats Crockett in Senate Race

articles.title: How Talarico Won Texas Democrats With Love, Luck and a Little Restraint
articles.description: A carefully disciplined campaign that capitalized on viral media, months of organizing and strong outreach to Latino voters helped propel James Talarico to the center of Texas politics.

articles.title: Gonzales is out in Texas, and Dems see a chance to beat ‘The AKGuy’
articles.description: Brandon Herrera’s controversial online presence and hardline politics could open another opportunity for House Democrats in a deep-red district.

articles.title: Dems aren’t sure whether to actually spend big to flip Texas
articles.description: National Democrats are worried the money it would take to compete in Texas could be better spent elsewhere.

articles.description: “She has been and has felt like a woman on an island,” says one operative.

articles.description: John Cornyn and his allies are trying to turn a surprising lead over firebrand Ken Paxton into a decisive presidential nod.

articles.description: In multiple Latino-majority counties, more people cast Democratic votes on Tuesday than voted for Kamala Harris in 2024.

articles.title: ‘A perfect storm is lining up for Texas Democrats’

articles.title: Cornyn is still in the fight. Will Trump finally endorse him?
articles.description: Even in the most heavily Republican counties where Attorney General Ken Paxton might have expected to benefit from a MAGA base, incumbent Sen. John Cornyn largely held his own.

articles.title: Talarico defeats Crockett in Texas Senate Democratic primary
articles.description: He took a big-tent approach to his campaign by appealing to voters from both parties and independents.

analysis.summary: James Talarico defeated Jasmine Crockett in the Texas Democratic Senate primary, running a big-tent, disciplined campaign buoyed by strong Latino voter turnout and viral media. On the Republican side, incumbent Sen. John Cornyn and Attorney General Ken Paxton remain locked in their own primary contest, with a potential Trump endorsement still pending. National Democrats are now debating whether to invest heavily in a Texas general election or allocate resources elsewhere.

Talarico ran a broad, big-tent campaign that appealed to voters across party lines and independents
Latino voter turnout was a major factor in Talarico's victory and in Democratic performance overall
